items.title: Neo-Nazi Thomas Sewell broadcasting hate on Instagram from Victorian acreage

children.content: ABC NEWS Verify can reveal that Neo-Nazi leader Thomas Sewell has been uploading antisemitic and homophobic diatribes to Instagram from a property on the outskirts of Melbourne.

gallery.altText: On the left, a man wearing a sparkling medieval mask. On the right a bald man with a moustache on the same background

thumbnail.altText: On the left, a man wearing a sparkling medieval mask. On the right a bald man with a moustache on the same background

items.title: Defence plans to sell Sydney Harbour land Albanese once fought to protect

children.content: Parliamentary speeches from 2021 reveal the prime minister was opposed to privatising Sydney Harbour land now being sold off by the Defence Department.

image.altText: A military base surrounded by Sydney Harbour

items.title: Another KPMG leader steps aside amid audit leak scandal fallout

children.content: KPMG Australia chief operating officer Eileen Hoggett will step aside from her role while investigations are pending into the audit leak scandal at the big four firm.

image.altText: Woman with dark-coloured hair cut into a bob stands in an office, smiling.

items.title: Parents of fit 27-year-old asked what quality of life he had by medical staff

children.content: Sam Stubbs’s parents were asked about his quality of life while treating him for a lung infection. They realised it was because he was born with Down syndrome.

gallery.altText: A young man dripping with sea water stands on a beach wearing a wetsuit

thumbnail.altText: A young man dripping with sea water stands on a beach wearing a wetsuit

items.title: Australia's economy growing at 2.5 per cent annually as slowdown begins

children.content: Australia's economy grew at an annual rate of 2.5 per cent in the March quarter, the same as in the previous quarter.

image.altText: People walking in Brisbane's Queen Street Mall

items.title: 'Too dangerous to push ahead': Aussie diver leaves Laos cave rescue

children.content: Josh Richards is on his way back to Australia, after multiple days helping rescue miners trapped in a Laos cave.

image.altText: A man with a beard and cap in a jungle camp

items.title: New Solomon Islands leader wants to 'reset' relations with Australia

children.content: The new Solomon Islands PM has made a clear pivot towards Australia, agreeing to kickstart negotiations on a comprehensive treaty and promising to "review" the Pacific nation's contentious security agreement with China.

image.altText: Two men in suits takl at a podium with an Australian flag behind them.

items.title: PM labels Taylor 'Temu Abbott' as Coalition turns up heat over economic growth

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese faces a grilling from the opposition over Australia's economy, following new economic figures showing a slowdown of growth. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: These are the 'worst offenders' in Australian packaging

children.content: A trendy plastic-and-metal takeaway cup used by cafes for iced drinks has been named Australia's worst packaging in the first Unpackit Awards.

gallery.altText: Four dirty, empty can-shaped plastic drinking vessels with a metal pull tab top.

thumbnail.altText: Four dirty, empty can-shaped plastic drinking vessels with a metal pull tab top.

items.title: 'You broke a tradition': Man fined for booing during Welcome to Country

children.content: Eli Toby told the court that his actions were intended as a way to "stand for what I believed was the right thing at the time", but refused to apologise to the judge.

image.altText: A man wearing a dark coat and a tie arrives at court.

items.title: Wife's lewd photo case against Luke Sayers to be heard in open court

children.content: A bid by Mr Sayers to have a defamation case brought by his wife over a nude photo scandal heard privately has been rejected by a judge.

image.altText: A bald man sits alone at a long table with a microphone. He is wearing a black suit, white collared shirt and purple tie.

items.title: Police investigating people entering New York sewers for unknown reason

children.content: Security cameras have recorded at least three night time instances where groups of people entered or exited sewer tunnels via maintenance holes on streets in Brooklyn and Queens.

image.altText: Three people descend through a manhole cover on a street in the dark

gallery.altText: three people descend into a sewer on a street in New York

thumbnail.altText: three people descend into a sewer on a street in New York

items.title: Iran launches ballistic missiles at American base as US hits Qeshm Island

children.content: The Revolutionary Guard says it has fired missiles and drones at US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, while the US military has launched an attack on Qeshm Island.

image.altText: a close up of a us military fighting falcon jet that is loaded with missiles

items.title: Gold reserves grow at Kalgoorlie's Super Pit

children.content: A big exploration push by Australia's biggest gold miner, Northern Star Resources, has grown the reserves at the Super Pit by 54 per cent in the past six years.

image.altText: A mining truck carrying gold-bearing ore.

items.title: Five ways to prevent mould in your home and the best ways to clean it

children.content: Winter is here and with it comes the increased risk of mould in homes, particularly in colder parts of the country. But there are simple ways to keep to it at bay.

gallery.altText: A gloved hand wipes a window sill with some mould in the cracks.

thumbnail.altText: A gloved hand wipes a window sill with some mould in the cracks.

items.title: Hollywood legend set to bring Finke Desert Race to the big screen

children.content: The gruelling outback Finke Desert Race is set for Hollywood treatment after Marvel's Chris Hemsworth backs a feature-length movie.

image.altText: Chris Hemsworth stares into the camera.

items.title: Why Hugh Jackman's brother cried on his first day as a prison-school teacher

children.content: After Ralph Jackman retrained as a teacher, his first job was in a youth detention centre. His new memoir reveals how a broken system is failing vulnerable kids.

image.altText: Three teenage boys sitting at desks in a classroom, facing the teacher, who is standing in front of a whiteboard.

items.title: Singer known for Disney's Aladdin hit duet A Whole New World dies

children.content: Peabo Bryson, known for his Grammy and Oscar-winning Disney collaborations, has died at 75.

gallery.altText: Peabo Bryson wearing a white suit and white framed reading glasses, holding a mic while surrounded by a crowd

thumbnail.altText: Peabo Bryson wearing a white suit and white framed reading glasses, holding a mic while surrounded by a crowd

items.title: Underworld figure Mick Gatto released without charge following arrest

children.content: Mick Gatto has been released without charge after being arrested by Victorian police investigating "alleged financial offences" earlier today.

image.altText: A man wearing a hat and bejewelled chain sits among a crowd

items.title: 'Perverse logic' over abortion, Barnaby Joyce tells Sydney crowd

children.content: Nine months out from the NSW election campaign, One Nation recruit Barnaby Joyce has appeared in Sydney, declaring politicians are fearful of losing their jobs.

image.altText: Mr Joyce wearing a suit and tie standing on a stage outside NSW parliament talking to a crowd at night.

items.title: Dramatic daylight arrest of NT man with police shooting front tyre of vehicle

children.content: Northern Territory police have carried out a dramatic arrest of a 33-year-old man on a main Darwin road, saying the fugitive was wanted for a breach of parole.

image.altText: Two police cars parked at a highway intersection in a suburban area.

items.title: Border Force busts NT multi-million-dollar illicit tobacco operation

children.content: Australian Border Force officers have seized hundreds of thousands of illegal cigarettes and vapes worth millions of dollars from a Northern Territory storage facility.

image.altText: A box with black packages with four cigarette packets lined up on top with one pack with cigarettes poking out of one packet

items.title: 'Us duck hunters': Victorian watchdog accused of siding with shooters

children.content: The Game Management Authority is accused of turning a blind eye to illegal behaviour by shooters while bullying wildlife rescuers and anti-hunting campaigners.

thumbnail.altText: A man with a shotgun over his shoulder carrying a gun in his other hand, up to his knees in water

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: Starmer condemns handcuffed teen's death as police response sparks protests

children.content: The British prime minister said he "felt sick" while watching video released by police of 18-year-old Henry Nowak, who was handcuffed while he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack.

image.altText: The heads and hands of protesters, including one holding a white sign with a picture of a Caucasian teen.

items.title: Trump names inexperienced ally as intelligence director

children.content: Bill Pulte, who is known for publicly attacking the US president's political enemies, would also retain his existing jobs overseeing federal housing and mortgage policies.

image.altText: A man in a suit smiles.

items.title: Banned products in Australia sold on Temu, Amazon and AliExpresss

children.content: Products that may already be banned in Australia are being sold by retail giants including Amazon, Temu, AliExpress and eBay.

image.altText: A bunch of toys, including fake cigarettes displayed with a white background

items.title: Snowy 2.0 blows out by more than $3m a day. Will it ever be worth it?

children.content: Australia's most ambitious renewable energy project is slowly nearing completion, but experts are divided on whether Snowy 2.0 will ever justify its snowballing price tag.

image.altText: A worker waters the ground in the distance while in a tunnel and a excavator works alongside them

items.title: No sign of missing Tasmanian devil Mary but experts confident it will survive

children.content: Sniffer dogs and thermal imaging drones are being deployed as the search for a missing Tasmanian devil on the Gold Coast goes into a second day.

gallery.altText: A man holds his hand over a black marsupial in a grassy area

thumbnail.altText: A man holds his hand over a black marsupial in a grassy area

items.title: Making sense of the latest BBL move that isn't 'a merger of the teams'

children.content: If the Renegades are sold to a private buyer as expected, what could it mean for Melbourne fans if the BBL team no longer looks like itself?

image.altText: Aaron Finch of the Melbourne Renegades waves during a BBL game.

items.title: Docker O'Driscoll says mental health admission 'best thing' he's done

children.content: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll says he has received "nothing but love" after posting on social media about his struggles with anxiety and how he contemplated taking his own life.

image.altText: Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll celebrates a goal with a shout in front of fans at Perth Stadium, in Fremantle's white jersey.

items.title: Paedophile magistrate's release prompts vigilante warning

children.content: South Australia's police chief says the force will respond accordingly to concerns about public safety once paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is released from prison, but added there would be no tolerance for "vigilante activity".

image.altText: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

items.title: No charges for man who coroner found faked wife's suicide

children.content: A man who a coroner found made his wife's 2010 death look like a suicide will not face prosecution after police said there was "insufficient evidence" to lay charges.

image.altText: Helen Bird smiling at the camera.

items.title: Robertson's Big Potato 'safe for the time being' amid court appeal

children.content: One of Australia's most infamous "big things" could be moved to make way for a new supermarket in the NSW Southern Highlands.
